headline.text: L3 found *SRS dark chocolate* in supersack during production — wrong product in ingredient, line now inspecting 100% of incoming truffles with OPL in place.

summary: Three sanitation gaps at L1 today: allergen test failure on S3 Tunnel 2 belt requiring rewash, uncovered flow wrap at S4, and wet contaminated area in S2 kitchen. L3 caught wrong-product chocolate in ingredient supersack and deployed 100% inspection. L2 dirty molds caused quality hold until replacement.

food_safety.title: Wrong product found in ingredient supersack
food_safety.summary: SRS dark chocolate piece found in MF Matrix supersack during production — OPL created, dedicated EE now inspecting 100% of product from supersack before use.
food_safety.status: Open, 100% inspection protocol active, only one supersack on line at a time.

food_safety.title: Pecan found in Tunnel 2 during visual inspection
food_safety.summary: Pecan piece and excessive grease found during S2 Tunnel 2 visual inspection at shift start — cleaning team addressed before production release.
food_safety.status: Resolved, tunnel re-cleaned and passed before line release.

food_safety.title: Flow wrap left uncovered overnight
food_safety.summary: Direct food-contact flow wrap material at S4 found uncovered and unprotected — contamination risk flagged by QA at 14:21.
food_safety.status: Resolved, material covered after QA intervention.

food_safety.title: Glycol contamination on finished product
food_safety.summary: Glycol found on eyes of smiling-face product at ML1 — source unknown, flagged at 14:52.
food_safety.status: Open, investigation needed on glycol source.

quality.title: Dirty molds causing surface defects at ML2
quality.summary: Bars coming out stained with holes due to dirty molds — line stopped at 15:08, team pulling and replacing all molds.
quality.status: Resolved, molds replaced and production resumed with acceptable quality.

quality.title: Underweight final parts on S3
quality.summary: Underweight pieces flagged at 22:57, operator making adjustments.
quality.status: Resolved on shift, operator adjusted and brought back into spec.

quality.title: Open pouches at FFS requiring blade cleaning
quality.summary: Recurring open-pouch issues during shift — line paused twice to clean blades, maintenance worked sealing adjustments.
quality.status: Resolved after blade cleaning and maintenance adjustment, packaging now acceptable.

quality.title: Open package spike on ML1 Cavanna
quality.summary: Open-package rate spiked at 14:19, filled one bin in 10 minutes — maintenance adjusted jaws cutting into chocolate, resolved by 14:50.
quality.status: Resolved after jaw adjustment, rate returned to acceptable levels.

sanitation.title: Allergen test failure on S3 Tunnel 2 belt
sanitation.summary: Tunnel 2 belt failed allergen test at 21:06 after wash cycle — belt rewashed 30 minutes, retested, passed at 21:45.
sanitation.status: Resolved, belt rewashed and passed allergen verification before production.

sanitation.title: Wet contaminated area in S2 kitchen
sanitation.summary: Wet area with rusty water under curtain next to S2 kitchen floor flagged by QA — contamination risk, requires curtain cut and dry-out.
sanitation.status: Open, waiting for deep clean and curtain replacement plan.

sanitation.title: Excessive grease on S2 Tunnel 2 table
sanitation.summary: Heavy grease buildup found during Tunnel 2 visual inspection at 11:55 — re-cleaned before production.
sanitation.status: Resolved, table re-cleaned and passed before line release.

sanitation.title: Unlabeled chemical barrels on floor
sanitation.summary: Two unlabeled barrels found on L2 floor at 16:46 — contents unknown, supervisor tasked to label and train team.
sanitation.status: Open, awaiting supervisor follow-up to identify and label.

opportunities.text: Consider adding verification step for flow wrap covering to S4 end-of-shift checklist — today's uncovered material sat overnight unprotected.
Review mold cleaning frequency and visual inspection trigger at ML2 — today's dirty molds caused surface defects and line stop.
Consider adding supersack inspection SOP and EE training at MF Matrix — today's wrong-product chocolate suggests receiving/staging gap.
Review mold tote segregation process at ML1 wash station — 60g molds found in 35g tote today, no tracking paperwork exists for mold washing.
